reason: Response A has the more controlled, organically developing essay structure characteristic of genuine Sonnet, with concrete sensory grounding ("thinning sheaf beneath your right thumb") and a single coherent arc rather than a list of discrete observations. Response B leans on punchier aphoristic one-liners ("It is the loudest thing in the book," "opens in only one direction") and a more formulaic paragraph-per-idea cadence that reads like an imitation amplifying Sonnet's surface mannerisms. B's opening claim ("happens nowhere else in human experience") is also an overstatement of a kind Sonnet tends to hedge.
